summary: Go-based GitHub reverse proxy service for HTTP/SSH GitHub access, releases, raw files, archives, gists, and API requests, with caching, rate limiting, authentication, metrics, and SSRF protection.
primary_use: Self-hosted GitHub reverse proxy for stable internal download acceleration
Archive zip/tar.gz fallback when git clone is not practical
Release/raw file proxying in controlled environments
Optional local/controlled proxy for teams or CI
recommended_agent_usage: Use gh-proxy archive URLs when clone is not possible and a public repository archive is acceptable.
Use for release/raw file downloads when a controlled self-hosted proxy is available.
Prefer localhost-only or authenticated deployments for sensitive usage.
Do not use public proxy instances for private repositories or token-bearing URLs.
capabilities: HTTP proxy for releases, raw files, archives, gists, and API endpoints
Full GitHub URL support and path-based URL support
Git HTTP and SSH proxy examples
Rate limiting, authentication, metrics, and security headers
SSRF protection and GitHub domain validation
limitations: Requires running a service and managing configuration
Exposing all interfaces without authentication can be risky
Private repository examples require token handling and should not be routed through public infrastructure
More operationally heavy than one-shot CLI helpers
security_notes: Bind to 127.0.0.1 or deploy behind a trusted reverse proxy when possible.
Enable authentication for shared or non-local deployments.
Never place GitHub PATs in public proxy URLs.
Use public proxies only for public open-source resources.
